content.value: To introduce someone else, you can use the phrase 呢位係... (ni1 wai2 hai6...), which means 'This person is...'. This is slightly more polite than just saying 'he/she is'.

content.romanization: ni1 wai2 hai6 wong4 siu2 ze2

content.value: In formal settings, you might hear 好榮幸識到你 (hou2 wing4 hang6 sik1 dou2 nei5), which is a polite way to say 'Very honored to meet you'.

content.romanization: hou2 wing4 hang6 sik1 dou2 nei5

lines.speaker: A
lines.romanization: can4 sin1 saang1, ni1 wai2 hai6 wong4 siu2 ze2.
lines.translation: Mr. Chan, this is Miss Wong.

lines.speaker: B
lines.cantonese: 黃小姐，你好。好榮幸識到你。
lines.romanization: wong4 siu2 ze2, nei5 hou2. hou2 wing4 hang6 sik1 dou2 nei5.
lines.translation: Miss Wong, hello. Very honored to meet you.

lines.speaker: C
lines.romanization: can4 sin1 saang1, nei5 taai3 haak3 hei3 laa3.
lines.translation: Mr. Chan, you're too kind / polite.

lines.speaker: A
lines.cantonese: 喂阿明，呢個係我朋友阿強。
lines.romanization: wai2 aa3 ming4, ni1 go3 hai6 ngo5 pang4 jau5 aa3 koeng4.
lines.translation: Hey Ah Ming, this is my friend Ah Keung.

lines.speaker: B
lines.romanization: nei5 hou2! aa3 koeng4, nei5 hou2 maa3?
lines.translation: Hello! Ah Keung, how are you?

pages.title: Lesson 2.4 Complete!
pages.summary: You've learned how to introduce others and use formal polite language in Cantonese.
